title: trichilia lepidota subsp. schumanniana
intro: Trichilia lepidota subsp. schumanniana, also known as the Schumann's Calabash Tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Meliaceae and is native to the tropical regions of West Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Trichilia lepidota subsp. schumanniana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
